news.summary: A new approach for primary care treatments now includes a blend of real-time prescription monitoring, unique BV solutions, and delivery through DoorDash®. Asembia, based in Florham Park, New Jersey, revealed its ASPN Digital Retail Network™ on April 16, 2025. This initiative aims to enhance primary care therapies by offering improved visibility into prescriptions, innovative BV solutions, and convenient DoorDash® delivery services.
news.headline: Asembia launches ASPN Digital Retail Network™ for enhanced prescription access, featuring real-time visibility and DoorDash® delivery.

news.summary: In Cary, North Carolina, on April 16, 2025, InstantGMP™, a top supplier of GMP-compliant software solutions for pharmaceutical and regulated manufacturing, has revealed its 5-Point Implementation Plan. This new strategy aims to facilitate a smooth onboarding process and assistance in the implementation of their services.
news.headline: InstantGMP™ launches 5-Point Implementation Plan for software success in pharmaceutical industry, aiming for streamlined onboarding.

news.summary: Josys, a SaaS Management platform based in Palo Alto, California, introduced a cutting-edge analytics dashboard on April 16, 2025. This dashboard is designed to enhance SaaS governance and boost IT efficiency by identifying and resolving security issues. The platform aims to streamline IT operations by providing comprehensive insights through this innovative tool. This development marks a significant advancement in the industry, emphasizing the importance of access automation in ensuring robust IT systems.
news.headline: Josys launches SaaS Management Scorecard, improves governance with analytics dashboard. Palo Alto, April 16, 2025.

news.summary: On April 16, 2025, Performance Shipping Inc. (NASDAQ: PSHG), a global tanker vessel company, announced the filing of its 2024 Annual Report on Form 20-F with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The report includes audited consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024. Shareholders can access the Annual Report on the SEC's website (http://www.sec.gov) and the company's website (http://www.pshipping.com). Upon request, shareholders can obtain a hard copy free of charge.
news.headline: Performance Shipping Inc. filed its 2024 Annual Report on Form 20-F with SEC for fiscal year ending Dec 31, 2024. Shareholders can access it online or request a hard copy for free.

news.summary: U.S. Bancorp (NYSE: USB) recently announced its first-quarter financial results, with earnings per share coming in at $1.03. This exceeded the analyst consensus of 98 cents. The company reported quarterly revenues of $6.95 billion, surpassing the estimated $6.91 billion. Adjusted net interest income saw a year-over-year increase of 2.7% to reach $4.092 billion. Additionally, the quarterly net interest margin stood at 2.72%, representing a 2-basis-point rise compared to the previous year. For further details, the complete news story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: U.S. Bancorp surpasses Q1 earnings estimates with $1.03 EPS, $6.95B revenues, and adjusted net interest income of $4.092B.

news.summary: Time magazine has recognized Tim Cadogan, CEO of GoFundMe, as one of the 100 Most Influential People of 2025. This acknowledgment highlights Tim’s innovative leadership in revolutionizing the way individuals support each other, utilizing technology to enhance communal assistance, kindness, and interpersonal relationships in the digital era. After assuming the role at GoFundMe in 2020, Tim solidified the organization's position as the foremost platform for contemporary philanthropy. In the past year alone, GoFundMe exceeded $30 billion in funds raised, showcasing its widespread impact.
news.headline: GoFundMe CEO Tim Cadogan named to TIME's 100 Most Influential People list for 2025, company raised over $30 billion.

news.summary: In San Francisco, Udemy, a prominent online skills marketplace and learning platform publicly traded on Nasdaq under the symbol UDMY, disclosed plans to reveal its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025 post the market closing on April 30, 2025. The company is scheduled to conduct a live conference call and webcast to analyze the outcomes on the same day at 2 p.m. PT / 5 p.m. ET. For access to earnings materials and recordings of the call, a link to the live webcast will be provided on the "Quarterly Re" section.

news.summary: A recent letter from the Rails to Trails Conservancy urges the U.S. Department of Transportation to safeguard federal funding for active transportation projects. The letter, supported by numerous officials across the political spectrum, including hundreds of mayors, county representatives, state legislators, and chambers of commerce, emphasizes the importance of maintaining these investments. This unified effort underscores the widespread bipartisan backing for the protection of federal resources in this area.
news.headline: Local officials and business leaders urge U.S. Department of Transportation to maintain federal investment in trails, walking, biking. Hundreds of mayors, county officials, state representatives, chambers of commerce show bipartisan support.

news.summary: Goldman Sachs analysts increased their earnings forecasts for top U.S. tech giants following a White House decision to exempt key electronics from steep tariffs on Chinese imports. The exemption, covering 20 tech product categories, includes items like smartphones, servers, routers, solid-state drives, and semiconductor equipment. The retroactive exemptions from April 5 aim to ease trade tensions for hardware manufacturers like Apple, Dell, HP Enterprise, and HP Inc. This reversal in tariff policy led to Goldman revising its estimates and predicting stronger profitability for these firms due to improved gross margins. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Goldman Sachs boosts forecasts for top U.S. tech giants like Apple, Dell, HP after tariffs exemption. Key electronics saved.

news.summary: Subaru has introduced its latest electric SUV, the 2026 Subaru Trailseeker EV, marking the brand's second battery-electric SUV. The vehicle features Subaru Symmetrical All-Wheel Drive, standard with 8.3 inches of ground clearance. It boasts impressive acceleration, going from 0-60 mph in approximately 4.4 seconds. The Trailseeker EV also offers a rapid charging capability, with speeds of up to an estimated 150 kW. Additionally, it comes equipped with a standard NACS-compatible charge port. The SUV has a towing capacity and promises an exciting blend of performance and eco-friendliness.
news.headline: Subaru unveils 2026 Trailseeker EV SUV with 150-kW charging speed, 4.4 sec 0-60mph, and 8.3 inches ground clearance.

news.summary: The latest Solterra XT model boasts a 25% range increase compared to its predecessors and is now furnished with a standard North American Charging Standard (NACS) port. Featuring an enhanced powertrain, the Solterra XT generates an impressive 338 combined horsepower. Additionally, charging time from 10-80% has been shortened to under 35 minutes, a result of significant improvements in the charging system.
news.headline: 2026 Subaru Solterra EV debuts with 25% more range, 338 horsepower, NACS port, and 35-min charging to 80%.

news.summary: In Hong Kong on April 14, 2025, Borqs Technologies, Inc. finalized the sale of its primary operations to Sasken Technologies, Ltd. The deal, valued at US$40 million, involved transferring all aspects of Borqs' embedded software design and custom hardware manufacturing for IoT ventures to Sasken. As part of the agreement, a portion of the sale proceeds will be allocated for staff, tax duties, and additional payments contingent on the company's performance in 2025. Borqs Technologies, Inc. is listed on the U.S. OTC market under the ticker symbol BRQSF, while Sasken Technologies, Ltd. trades on the Indian NSE and BSE under the symbols SASKEN and 532663 respectively.
news.headline: Borqs Technologies sells core businesses to Sasken Technologies for $40 million; deal includes IoT products division and earnout clause.

news.summary: The implementation of Trump's tariffs has disrupted the plans of numerous companies looking to go public and has also hindered their ability to make financial decisions. The ongoing issue seems to have no clear resolution in the near future, causing uncertainty among businesses.
news.headline: Trump's tariffs disrupt Wall Street as companies struggle to go public or make financial decisions, uncertainty persists.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures saw a rise on Monday after Friday's gains, with major indices showing higher premarket numbers. President Trump exempted smartphones, computers, and chips from new reciprocal tariffs on Chinese goods, leaving a 20% tariff on all other items. Commerce Secretary hinted at possible future tariffs. Bank earnings and lower-than-expected inflation data were also considered by investors. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.46% and the two-year bond at 3.92%. Futures showed changes: Dow Jones +1.00%, S&P 500 +1.44%, Nasdaq 100 +1.69%, Russell 2000 +0.95%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF rose in premarket. In the last session, U.S. stocks saw gains fueled by bank earnings and cooler inflation data, with weekly gains for S&P 500 (5.7%), Nasdaq (7.3%), and Dow (~5%). Key numbers from the previous session: S&P 500 at 5,363.36, Nasdaq Composite at 16,724.46, Dow Jones at 40,212.71, and Russell 2000 at 1,860.20. Louis Navellier suggested caution amid market volatility.
news.headline: US stock futures are up, with potential for a higher open following exemptions of electronics from tariffs. Dow Jones +1.00%, S&P 500 +1.44%, Nasdaq 100 +1.69%, Russell 2000 +0.95%. Positive sentiment from bank earnings & low inflation data. Major averages saw significant weekly gains: S&P 500 +5.7%, Nasdaq +7.3%, Dow +5%. Dow Jones at 40,212.71, S&P 500 at 5,363.36, Nasdaq at 16,724.46, Russell 2000 at 1,860.20.

news.summary: Legacy media outlets and a small group of writers are stirring up controversy surrounding a question and positive remarks from Washington, D.C. Chief Correspondent Cara Castronuova during a White House press briefing on Friday. The incident left some reporters laughing while others were shocked and unhappy. This has sparked debate within the journalism community, with a few individuals attempting to amplify the situation.
news.headline: Mike Lindell Media Corp. reporter Cara Castronuova stirs controversy with a question on President Trump's health in White House press briefing, causing journalists to laugh and others to be upset.

news.summary: In Jacksonville, Florida, Dream Finders Homes, Inc. achieved significant growth with strong acquisitions and a record-high revenue, leading to the prestigious recognition as the 2025 Builder of the Year by Zonda's BUILDER. The CEO, Patrick Zalupski, attributed much of this success to the dedicated team spread across various regions in the U.S. The company's exceptional performance was highlighted by their successful acquisitions and unprecedented revenue figures. Zalupski emphasized the integral role of the talented individuals within the company in driving their growth plans forward.
news.headline: Dream Finders Homes wins 2025 Builder of the Year Award for record revenue and strong acquisitions; CEO credits team.

news.summary: A partnership has been formed between Acclaro and Unbabel to enhance global AI-powered translation services. The collaboration aims to combine their expertise in translation technologies to provide more efficient and accurate translation solutions worldwide. This partnership is expected to benefit numerous clients by offering high-quality translation services with improved speed and accuracy.
news.headline: Acclaro & Unbabel team up to advance global AI translation services, boosting capabilities.

news.summary: Apple and other tech companies have been granted a temporary exemption from many of the tariffs imposed by the Trump administration. Although this news caused a rise in Dow Jones Futures, President Trump emphasized that this exemption is only temporary. This development is speculated to have a positive impact on Apple and other tech companies, with Palantir also making a bullish move in the market. This move in the market has been seen as a significant development by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Dow Jones futures rise on Trump tariff news for Apple and tech companies, Palantir makes bullish move.

news.summary: The stock of TSLA is currently close to its lowest point in 2025 as the company prepares to announce its Q1 earnings. This comes at a time when the brand of Tesla and its CEO, Elon Musk, are reportedly facing challenges. In contrast, BYD's earnings are on the rise, supported by various factors driving growth. The comparison between Tesla and BYD highlights the impact of Tesla's brand struggles as its competitor in fast-charging technologies experiences a surge in success.
news.headline: Tesla's brand suffers as TSLA stock nears 2025 lows. Q1 earnings awaited amid Elon Musk controversy. BYD earnings surge.

news.summary: In 2025, Virtual YouTubers (VTubers) have become popular digital content creators using computer-generated avatars. The market was valued at $2.55 billion in 2024 and is projected to hit $20 billion by 2035. Becoming a VTuber involves designing a unique avatar, using motion capture for animation, and leveraging software like Live2D and VSeeFace. Success often starts on platforms like TikTok and YouTube Shorts, with cross-posting to platforms like Discord and Twitch aiding in community building and monetization. VTubers face risks such as burnout, privacy breaches, platform dependency, and unpredictable income. The industry is evolving, with trends like niche content, 2D esthetics, focus on culture and uniqueness, and the use of NFTs emerging in 2025. However, creators must also be cautious of challenges like burnout, privacy threats, platform dependency, monetization difficulties, high upfront costs, intense competition, and AI impersonation. Success as a VTuber requires unique branding, technical polish, and dedication.
news.headline: In 2025, VTubing combines real people with digital avatars, using motion capture and software tools to create content and earn income on platforms like TikTok and YouTube. The $2.55 billion VTuber market is projected to reach $20.0 billion by 2035. Advancements in technology have led to more tools and trends in the VTuber space, but creators should be aware of risks like burnout, privacy breaches, and platform dependency.

news.summary: In Pittsburgh, PPG (NYSE: PPG) recently elevated scientists David Fenn and Renhe Lin to the esteemed status of PPG associate fellow. This title is highly regarded in the scientific realm and is bestowed upon PPG employees in the science and technology field who demonstrate exceptional innovation and possess promising future potential. These promotions are a significant milestone within the PPG technical organization, highlighting the commitment to excellence and forward-thinking approach of the company's employees.
news.headline: PPG promotes David Fenn and Renhe Lin to associate fellows, recognizing their technical achievements. Prestigious designation in the scientific community.

news.summary: Prologis Inc. (NYSE: PLD) shares are on the rise following the release of its first-quarter FY25 earnings results. The company reported rental and other revenue of $2.00 billion, falling short of the analyst consensus estimate of $2.04 billion. Core FFO came in at $1.42, surpassing the analyst consensus estimate of $1.38. Owned & Managed Average Occupancy reached 94.9%, while Prologis Share Average Occupancy was slightly lower at 94.8%. Prologis' Share of Same Store Property NOI - Cash experienced a 6.2% year-over-year increase to $1.35 billion. Total expenses for the quarter rose to $1.33 billion compared to $1.29 billion in the same quarter last year. For more details, please refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Prologis beats Q1 FFO estimate, same-store NOI up 6.2% Y/Y. Revenue at $2B, Core FFO at $1.42. Occupancy 94.9%. Total expenses rise to $1.33B.

news.summary: In Dublin on April 16, 2025, Medius, a renowned company offering AP Automation and Spend Management solutions, disclosed the selection of John Seery as its new Chief Technology Officer (CTO). This move underscores Medius' dedication to boosting innovation utilizing AI technology and spearheading advancements in the field.
news.headline: Medius appoints John Seery as CTO to drive innovation, focusing on AI. Leading provider of AP Automation.

news.summary: In Baltimore, TNF Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: TNFA) presented on "Isomyosamine for the Treatment of Sarcopenia in Older Adults" at the British Geriatrics Society (BGB) Spring Meeting 2025. TNF, a biopharmaceutical company focusing on autoimmune and inflammatory conditions, showcased the abstract delivered by Mitchell Glass, M.D., the President, and Chief Medical Officer. The presentation discussed novel therapies for sarcopenia in older adults.TNF is committed to developing treatments for such conditions.
news.headline: TNF Pharmaceuticals presents oral TNF-Alpha Inhibitor Isomyosamine at BGB Spring Meeting 2025 by Dr. Glass.

news.summary: In Center Valley, Pennsylvania, Shift4 (NYSE: FOUR), a prominent provider of integrated payments and commerce technology, has disclosed details regarding its first quarter 2025 financial results. The company is set to unveil its Q1 2025 financial outcomes before the market opens on Tuesday, April 29, 2025. In tandem with this release, Shift4's management will conduct a conference call at 8:30am ET on the same day to discuss these results. Additionally, Shift4 will be participating in upcoming investor conferences.
news.headline: Shift4 (NYSE: FOUR) announces Q1 2025 financial results release on April 29, 2025. Earnings conference call at 8:30am ET.

news.summary: Abu Dhabi, in the United Arab Emirates, saw a groundbreaking event where Artificial Intelligence emerged victorious in the world's most advanced autonomous drone race. The competition showcased cutting-edge technology and innovation, with impressive numbers of participants and spectators. The event highlighted the increasing role of AI in various industries and its potential for revolutionizing autonomous systems.
news.headline: AI wins autonomous drone race in Abu Dhabi, showcasing cutting-edge tech.

news.summary: Cadence, based in San Jose, California, has recently announced a significant deal to acquire Arm's Artisan foundation IP business. The company has entered into a definitive agreement with Arm for this acquisition. The deal involves Cadence purchasing a substantial portion of Arm's IP business assets. This acquisition is expected to boost Cadence's capabilities and intellectual property portfolio significantly. The deal underscores the strategic growth plan of Cadence and is set to have a notable impact on the industry. The financial specifics of the agreement, including the exact amount involved, have not been disclosed publicly.
news.headline: Cadence to buy Arm's Artisan foundation IP business in definitive deal. #CDNS #Arm #acquisition

This emblem is widely acknowledged for signifying superior quality and serves as a testament to the excellent standards upheld by CertainTeed.
news.headline: CertainTeed's entire residential roofing shingle portfolio earns Good Housekeeping Seal, symbol of quality.

news.summary: Today, in Memphis, Tennessee on April 16, 2025, First Horizon Corporation (NYSE: FHN), a financial services company, faced an unexpected issue with an external carrier which led to a change in the Conference Call Dial-in information for their meeting scheduled for 8:30 AM CT. The new details for accessing the conference call have been provided.
news.headline: First Horizon Corporation's conference call at 8:30 AM CT has updated dial-in information due to an external carrier outage.

news.summary: An extensive study conducted by Ballotpedia has analyzed the authority of local school boards in all 50 states. The research delves into state laws and regulations that both empower and restrict local school boards. This comprehensive resource provides valuable insights for education policymakers and stakeholders. The study, recently concluded in Middleton, Wisconsin on April 16, 2025, offers a detailed examination of the varying degrees of authority granted to local school boards nationwide. This invaluable resource sheds light on the complexities of governance in education, impacting countless schools and communities.
news.headline: Ballotpedia analyzed school board authority in all 50 states to see how laws affect their powers.

news.summary: In Skokie, Illinois, Synapse Health has appointed Mandira Singh as president, a move aimed at advancing the durable medical equipment (DME) industry towards value-based care. Singh, who initially served as a senior advisor starting in August, is now part of an expanding executive team dedicated to modernizing the DME ecosystem for improved service to patients, prescribers, and DME provider partners. This step underscores the company's commitment to integrating tech products effectively within the industry.
news.headline: Synapse Health appoints Mandira Singh as President, aiming to modernize DME industry for better patient care.

news.summary: Veeco Instruments Inc., a company listed on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol VECO, is set to announce its financial results for the first quarter of 2025 on May 7, 2025. The announcement will be made after the market closes on that day. Following the release, the company will conduct a conference call to discuss these results at 5:00 PM ET. This event is open for investors, analysts, and other interested parties to participate in the review of Veeco Instruments Inc.'s financial performance for the first quarter of 2025.
news.headline: Veeco will release Q1 2025 results on May 7, after market close, with conference call at 5:00 PM ET.

news.summary: An advanced platform has been developed to assist customs authorities in enhancing their ability to identify goods and ensure trade compliance through the use of artificial intelligence technology. This forward-looking platform aims to empower customs officials with efficient tools for goods identification and trade regulations enforcement. By leveraging AI-driven algorithms, this platform offers a cutting-edge solution for customs agencies to streamline their processes and increase accuracy in identifying and classifying goods. This innovative system is set to revolutionize the way customs operations are carried out, potentially reducing errors and enhancing efficiency. With a focus on empowering customs with advanced tools, this platform is poised to make a significant impact on trade compliance globally.
news.headline: ScanTech AI Systems to launch CustomsTrace AI™ for goods verification in changing tariffs. AI aids customs for trade compliance.

news.summary: Agoura Hills, California - April 16, 2025 - Oncotelic Therapeutics, Inc (OTCQB:OTLC) revealed its financial performance for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024, in comparison to the prior fiscal year ending December 31, 2023. The company, specializing in treatments for rare diseases like Parkinson's Disease, pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma (DIPG), and various cancers, shared this data. The details were outlined in the Annual Report on Form 10-K submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission on April 15, 2025.
news.headline: Oncotelic Therapeutics reports FY 2024 highlights & earnings compared to FY 2023, based on Form 10-K filed with SEC.

news.summary: The scalable rollout aims to generate annual revenue ranging from USD2 to 4 million within the initial year.
news.headline: Treasure Global Inc. to launch Mezzofy's $40B coupon platform in Malaysia, aiming for $2-4M annual revenue in first year.

news.summary: Join us for a live moderated webcast featuring key members of the Senti Bio management team on Wednesday, April 23rd at 4:00 PM Eastern Time. Tune in for an exclusive discussion with company executives about the latest developments and future plans of Senti Bio. Don't miss this opportunity to gain valuable insights directly from the management team. Save the date!
news.headline: Senti Bio (NASDAQ: SNTI) will have a live virtual investor segment on April 23rd at 4:00 PM ET.

news.summary: Beijing, April 16, 2025 - Kingsoft Cloud Holdings Limited, a prominent cloud service provider in China, revealed the details of its recent underwritten public offering. The offering consisted of 18,500,000 American depositary shares (ADSs), with each ADS representing 15 ordinary shares of the Company. The pricing was set at US$11.27 per ADS and HK$5.83 per ordinary share, totaling 277,500,000 ordinary shares based on the exchange rate of HK$7.7574 to US$1.00 at the time of pricing. All ADSs were offered by Kingsoft Cloud, and investors had the option to receive ordinary shares of the Company for trading on the HKEX instead of ADSs in the offering.
news.headline: Kingsoft Cloud prices public equity offering at US$11.27 per ADS, totaling 18,500,000 ADSs and $277,500,000 ordinary shares.

news.summary: Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) saw a rise in premarket trading as it reported sales of $11.81 billion, marking a 5% increase from last year and surpassing the analyst consensus of $10.81 billion. The company exceeded expectations with first-quarter adjusted EPS of $1.91 compared to the estimated $0.80. Despite this, core income dropped by 60% year over year to $443 million due to higher catastrophe losses. The company faced catastrophe losses of $2.266 billion pre-tax, mostly from the California wildfires in January 2025. Additionally, they experienced net realized investment losses of $61 million pre-tax ($48 million after-tax) in contrast to gains in the previous year quarter. These details and more can be found on Benzinga.com, where an analyst highlighted Travelers Companies' scale and analytical advantages, making it an attractive option for investors.
news.headline: Travelers beats Q1 estimates with $11.81 billion in sales (+5% YoY), $1.91 EPS, but 60% income drop due to $2.266 billion wildfire losses.

news.summary: A company based in Barrington, Rhode Island, SageX, revealed on April 16, 2025, that it has been recognized as a Finalist at the esteemed 2025 EdTech Awards. SageX specializes in offering an AI-driven mobile platform designed for workforce e-coaching and performance enhancement. This platform focuses on professional development and has gained significant recognition in the industry.
news.headline: SageX is a finalist in the 2025 EdTech Cool Tool Awards for their AI-powered mobile platform for professional development.

news.summary: Expanding its presence in the high science community, a central hub has been launched for enthusiasts to watch TV episodes, view behind-the-scenes content, purchase exclusive merchandise, access the High Science Charity Foundation, and stay updated on industry news. A free app is available for download on the Apple App Store, and more information can be found at HighScience.com. This initiative is based in Scottsdale, Arizona, and was announced on April 16, 2025.
news.headline: High Science® launches official app for community, includes TV episodes, exclusive merch, charity access. App available for free on Apple App Store.

news.summary: In Columbus, Ohio, 18 independent coffee shops are marking anniversaries this April with support from Crimson Cup Coffee & Tea. The cafes have been in operation for between one and 24 years, showcasing the impact of Crimson Cup's 7 Steps program.
news.headline: 18 independent coffee shops supported by Crimson Cup are celebrating anniversaries, ranging from 1 to 24 years.

news.summary: In a recent appearance on CNN, Toy Association President and CEO Greg Ahearn raised concerns about President Trump's implementation of high tariffs on Chinese imports, warning that it could potentially disrupt the upcoming holiday season. Ahearn highlighted the fact that a significant portion of the world's toys are manufactured in China, rendering the U.S. supply chain unprepared for the repercussions of these tariffs. He stated that there is currently a halt in toy production in China, leading to U.S. retailers starting to cancel their orders. Ahearn emphasized that the majority of toy manufacturers in the U.S. are small or mid-sized businesses, placing them at risk due to these developments. For more information, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Toy Industry CEO warns Trump's tariffs may hinder holiday season, as China produces majority of toys. U.S. supply chain unprepared. U.S. retailers already canceling orders from China. Domestic toy makers face challenges.
SUNation Energy implements a reverse stock split to consolidate shares.
